Clara to handle the call, collect information, and Maria will call back.

v2_value: For existing customers asking for Tony specifically, transfer to Tony's cell (813-555-0167).

v1_value: Uses Connectwise Manage for ticketing and project management, but currently has no API integrations for it. Clara should not attempt direct integration with Connectwise Manage at this time.
v2_value: Uses Connectwise Manage for ticketing and project management.
No API integration with Clara needed for Connectwise Manage.
Clara should never try to create tickets or access Connectwise.
Clara should NEVER ask for or record alarm codes, passwords, or access credentials. If a client has a code-related question, Clara should note it and route to Maria or Tony.

Service area is the Tampa Bay metropolitan area (Tampa, St. Petersburg, Clearwater, Brandon, and surrounding Hillsborough and Pinellas counties). Calls from outside this area (e.g., Miami, Jacksonville) cannot be serviced.

changes.v1_value: During after-hours (evenings, weekends, and holidays), Clara will first determine if the call is an emergency based on defined triggers (fire alarm malfunctions, alarm panel down, security system failures at high-risk facilities, or critical monitoring partner calls). If it's an emergency, Clara will follow the three-tier escalation protocol: first attempting to reach Maria (813-555-0145), then Tony (813-555-0167), and finally Pete (813-555-0189). If the call is not an emergency, Clara will inform the caller that the office is closed, collect their name, phone number, and reason for calling, and assure them that someone will call them back on the next business day. Weekends are specifically reserved for emergency callbacks only.
changes.v2_value: During after-hours, if a call is identified as an emergency based on the defined triggers, Clara will initiate a three-tier escalation process: first calling Maria (813-555-0145) for 30 seconds, then Tony (813-555-0167) for 30 seconds, and finally Pete (813-555-0189) for 30 seconds. If no one picks up across all three tiers, Clara will take a detailed message, including the urgency level, and send text alerts to all three contacts. For non-emergency calls after hours, Clara will take a message, collecting the caller's name, company, phone number, system type, and what service they need. The caller will be informed that Coastal Alarm Systems will follow up by 10 AM the next business day.

changes.v1_value: During office hours (Monday-Friday, 8 AM-5 PM Eastern Time), Clara will screen incoming calls. If the call is identified as an emergency (e.g., fire alarm malfunction, security system failure at high-risk facility, critical monitoring partner call), Clara will immediately initiate the emergency routing protocol: first attempting to reach Maria (813-555-0145), then Tony (813-555-0167), and finally Pete (813-555-0189). For non-emergency calls, such as requests for new installation quotes or scheduling service visits, Clara will collect the caller's name, phone number, and the nature of their inquiry, and take a message for Maria or other staff to address during business hours. Clara will handle monitoring company calls professionally, adapting conversation style as needed.
changes.v2_value: During business hours (Monday-Friday, 8 AM-5 PM ET), all incoming calls are initially transferred to Maria (813-555-0145). If Maria is busy and does not answer within 20 seconds, Clara will handle the call by collecting the necessary information, and Maria will call the client back. For existing customers who specifically ask to speak with Tony, Clara will transfer the call directly to Tony's cell (813-555-0167). Calls from monitoring companies are considered high priority; Clara will attempt to transfer these calls to Maria immediately, and if Maria is unavailable, the call will be transferred to Tony.

v1_value: Coastal Alarm Systems does not work weekends unless it is an emergency callback.
v2_value: Coastal Alarm Systems observes major holidays but maintains flexibility for high-priority clients requiring emergency service.
The business exclusively handles commercial work; no residential services are provided.
Electrical work is limited to low-voltage systems; services like new panel or outlet installation are not offered.
The company does not sell or service safes, nor do they provide locksmithing services.

Clara is strictly prohibited from asking for or recording any alarm codes, passwords, or access credentials due to security protocols. Any related inquiries should be noted and routed to Maria or Tony.

Coastal Alarm Systems operates within the Tampa Bay metropolitan area, including Tampa, St. Petersburg, Clearwater, Brandon, and surrounding Hillsborough and Pinellas counties. Calls from outside this service area will not be accommodated.
Coastal Alarm Systems is licensed in Florida for alarm contractor work under State License EF20001567, which Clara can reference if asked.

changes.v1_value: Coastal Alarm Systems is a commercial security and fire alarm system installer and service provider based in Tampa, Florida. The company has been in business for 6 years and has grown from 5 to 18 employees in the last two years. They specialize in commercial fire alarm systems, security alarm systems, access control, CCTV, alarm monitoring coordination, and low-voltage wiring. Currently, their receptionist Maria handles calls during business hours, while after-hours calls are forwarded to the owner Tony's cell phone, which he finds disruptive. They use Connectwise Manage for ticketing and project management but do not have any API integrations. The pricing for Clara's software was not discussed in this transcript, only that a proposal would be sent. The onboarding call is scheduled for 'next week'. No mention of Jobber or QuickBooks usage. Tony's cell phone (813-555-0167) currently serves as the primary after-hours contact, which Clara will now manage through the emergency routing.
changes.v2_value: Updated with onboarding data. Operational details confirmed.
